title: VORTEX SHEDDING
preview: Today, I had two kayaks strapped on top of my car, remnants of the weekend trek to Stallings island to go find and feed the mythic donkeys. As I turned onto an interstate road, I...
content:
Today, I had two kayaks strapped on top of my car, remnants of the weekend trek to Stallings island to go find and feed the mythic donkeys. As I turned onto an interstate road, I was faced with an unforeseen challenger, a 55mph speed limit. “Surely, the straps would hold,” I thought to myself. As my foot pressed on the accelerator, I watched the tip of the kayaks from the windscreen. They remained in place, but a loud groan began to perpetuate outside my window.

These signals triggered a commencement of mini experiments. First, I began with acceleration and deceleration, and as I had suspected, the groans playfully matched the velocity of my car. Then, I began to question the tautness of the straps. “Perhaps they were too loose?” I thought. Above my head, I pulled on the straps. Instead of resolving the groans, I heard an increase in pitch. Curious. I wondered if I could identify other metrics through touch and opened the window on the driver’s side to reach my hand up towards the strap. I felt a strong vibration, which upon my touch, redirected its energy from the strap to my hand, slightly reducing the incongruent noises. I decided it was probably a good idea for me to stop. Pulling into a gas station, I whipped out my phone and asked Google’s gemini about the phenomenon of groaning kayak straps to which it quickly responded with a term called “vortex shedding” and the solution being twisting the parts of the straps not directly touching the kayaks. With a quick confirmation from the trusty source of Reddit, I twisted in a few turns and proceeded onto the road. To my delight, the suggestion worked and I was met with the absence of the groaning vibrations.

Later that day, I began my spiral down vortex shedding, a phenomenon (probably understood well by aerospace nerds) in which wind passing by flat straps induces vibrations. As air is separated by the straps, whirlpools of low pressure form on each side of the strap in an alternating fashion leading to this high-speed vibration resulting in acoustic resonance. According to gemini, “every taut object has a natural frequency at which it wants to vibrate when disturbed (like a tuned guitar string) and when these frequencies align (frequency of shedding vortices and frequency of tight strap), resonance occurs.”

Now this is extremely curious to me. Not in the context of physics, but in the context of relationships. It reminded me of a recent encounter I had with an individual who was scarily similar in their internally construction and that when two people of the same frequency connect, it has the capacity to build resonance. However, much like the strap, if done at such uncontrolled intensity for prolonged periods of time, it may result in irrecoverable damage. Thus, resonance, something that can produce sound in a strap and love between two individuals, must be bounded and contained to preserve the integrity of both interacting structures. On another note, one can refuse to embody the fullness of who they are as an independent entity. An identity is never explored. The strap remains slack. It is indeed safe. But it comes at the cost of never truly producing resonance.
